name: Breaking Bad
description: Walter White, Jesse Pinkman, and the Albuquerque drug empire

questions.q: Who created 'Breaking Bad'?

source.notes: Also co-created the prequel 'Better Call Saul' with Peter Gould.

questions.q: Which actor plays high school chemistry teacher Walter White?

source.notes: Won 4 Emmys for the role. Previously known for 'Malcolm in the Middle.'

questions.q: Which actor portrays Jesse Pinkman?

source.notes: Character was originally supposed to die at the end of Season 1. Won 3 Emmys.

questions.q: In which U.S. city is 'Breaking Bad' set?

source.notes: Also filmed there. New Mexico state flag colors (red/yellow) subtly used in Walt's wardrobe.

questions.q: What subject does Walter White teach before entering the meth business?

source.notes: Former researcher who co-founded Gray Matter Technologies before dropping out.

questions.q: What is Walter White's drug-dealer alias?

source.notes: Named after physicist Werner Heisenberg, famous for the uncertainty principle.

questions.q: What distinctive color is Walt and Jesse's signature methamphetamine?

source.notes: Scientifically impossible for actual meth to be pure blue, but made for iconic TV.

questions.q: Which fried chicken chain serves as the front for Gus Fring's drug operation?

source.notes: 'The Chicken Brothers' in Spanish. Distinctive yellow and red logo.

questions.q: Which actor plays the smooth-talking criminal lawyer Saul Goodman?

source.notes: Character got his own prequel series, 'Better Call Saul' (2015-2022).

questions.q: What federal agency does Walt's brother-in-law Hank Schrader work for?

source.notes: Drug Enforcement Administration. Played by Dean Norris.

questions.q: What medical condition is Walter White diagnosed with in the series pilot?

source.notes: Inoperable lung cancer. Motivates him to secure money for his family.

questions.q: How old is Walter White when he begins cooking meth in the pilot?

source.notes: Turns 50 in the pilot. The flash-forward 'bacon' scenes are at age 52.

questions.q: Which kleptomaniac character is Hank's wife and Skyler's sister?

source.notes: Played by Betsy Brandt. Obsessed with the color purple.

questions.q: Which character is introduced as a no-nonsense problem-solver and Mike Ehrmantraut's employer?

source.notes: Played by Giancarlo Esposito. Owner of Los Pollos Hermanos.

questions.q: What front business does Walt eventually use to launder money?

source.notes: Purchased from Walt's old boss Bogdan. Walt punches the 'lucky' air freshener off the wall.

questions.q: Which comedian plays Saul's fixer Patrick Kuby?

source.notes: Burr is also a regular guest on 'Better Call Saul.'

questions.q: What massive weapon does Walt rig up in the trunk of his car to use in the finale?

source.notes: Mounted on a trunk-based motorized swivel. Used to kill Jack's neo-Nazi gang.

questions.q: What does Walter White Jr. briefly rename himself in the show?

source.notes: Rejects his father's name. Played by RJ Mitte, who has cerebral palsy in real life.

questions.q: Who shoots and kills the child witness Drew Sharp during the train heist?

source.notes: Todd Alquist, played by Jesse Plemons. Part of Uncle Jack's crew.

questions.q: Gus Fring's backstory reveals he originally came from which country?

source.notes: His full identity is kept mysterious; Chile is stated but never verified by Mexican cartel contacts.
